business.headline: Buying insurance online? 8 in 10 users say it can lead to problemsA nationwide survey has found that more than 80% of users faced issues such as hidden charges, difficulty with cancelling policies, forced data sharing and misleading pricing while using online insurance platforms.

Rupee hits all-time low, slips past Rs 93 mark against US dollarThe rupee weakened to Rs 93.15 against the US dollar in early trade, extending recent losses amid rising geopolitical tensions and a surge in crude oil prices.

Sensex jumps 700 points: Why is stock market rising today? 3 key reasonsThe rebound comes as easing oil prices, positive global cues, strong buying in IT stocks, and broad-based buying after Thursday's sharp fall lifted investor sentiment.

Is Dubai's luxury housing boom starting to crack as Gulf conflict escalates?Dubai's property market faces a key test as tensions rise, sentiment weakens, and luxury villa deals come under pressure, with the outlook hinging on how demand and geopolitical risks evolve.

US issues fresh 30-day sanctions waiver for purchase of Russian oil at seaThe license, which expires on April 11, replaces and supersedes a similar 30-day sanctions waiver issued on March 12.

US says it may lift sanctions on Iranian oil stranded in tankers to calm pricesUS Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the addition of sanctioned Iranian oil into global supplies would help keep oil prices down for the next 10 to 14 days. Oil prices have been above $100 per barrel for much of the past two weeks as Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz to shipping and has attacked tankers.

Iran may impose transit fees on ships to pass through Strait of HormuzSince the start of the US-Israeli war on Iran, Tehran has disrupted maritime transit through the strait for vessels it says are linked to its war adversaries and their allies.

Gold down nearly Rs 7,000, silver drops Rs 20,000: Buy, sell or hold now?Gold and silver prices plunged sharply today, with gold losing around Rs 7,000 and silver dropping Rs 20,000. This fall has buyers and investors debating whether to invest now or wait for a recovery.

Choice Mutual Fund rolls out two new fund offerings. Details hereThe two new schemes, i.e., Choice Nifty 50 Index Fund and Choice Nifty Next 50 Index Fund, are designed to give investors exposure to India's equity market without the complexity of actively managed funds.

Sensex ends 2,500 points lower, Nifty below 23,100; HDFC down 5%The S&P BSE Sensex plummeted 2,496.89 points to close at 74,207.24, while the NSE Nifty50 tanked 775.65 points to end at 23,002.15.

How the Hormuz disruption could make South Mumbai flats more expensiveA ripple from far beyond India's shores is beginning to touch Mumbai's skyline. If you're planning to buy a South Mumbai flat, you may soon notice prices inching higher.

How Iran is making Wall Street bleed in retaliation to US strikesSince February 27, all three major US indices have declined. The Dow Jones Industrial Average has fallen about 2,752 points, or around 5.6%. The S&P 500 is down about 254 points, or 3.7%, while the Nasdaq Composite has dropped around 515 points, or 2.3%.

Is crypto the new market favourite amid ongoing West Asia conflict?As tensions in West Asia push oil prices higher and keep global markets on edge, investors are once again looking for safety. But this time, alongside gold and the dollar, crypto is gaining attention — is it becoming the new market favourite? Let's have a look.

Live: Another blow for Iran as Revolutionary Guards Spokesperson killed in strikesUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Iran struck major energy sites in Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Kuwait, triggering fires and sending oil prices sharply higher, with Brent crude up more than 60% since the war began. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Iran can no longer enrich uranium or build missiles, and confirmed Israel would pause further strikes on Iranian gas fields at US President Donald Trump's request. The Pentagon is seeking an additional $200 billion in war funding. Casualties continue to mount, with deaths reported in Iran, Israel, Lebanon and the West Bank, and over one million people displaced in Lebanon alone. More than 1,300 people in Iran have been killed during the war and at least 13 US military members have been killed. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Watch: 1,000-km-long rain band covers India, Pakistan, AfghanistanThis straight formation is very rare, unlike the usual curved troughs seen in such systems. Such shape and straight structure often builds up into intense thunderstorms with heavy rain, lightning, and strong gusty winds.

16% of world's AI workforce is now in IndiaThe world's 16% AI workforce is now in India. Organisations are rapidly adopting AI, and colleges are doing the same. This upward rise is placing India among the leading nations advancing in this space.
